behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff across modules (file load + env override + validation + error formatting). A Unit test of only validator logic can miss a real failure where merge/lookup drops nesting and reports `host` instead of `database.host`. Integration-brake: no real network/subprocess/OS-only primitive is required, and no AI output is involved, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader end-to-end in-process with a temp config file missing `database.host` and no matching `TOOL_` env var; assert startup fails and error contains exact path `database.host`. Concrete miss if only Unit: path gets flattened during merge, validator unit still passes on direct dict, but real loader reports wrong key path.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about wiring between env resolution and actual config file selection. A Unit test of `resolve_path()` alone can miss a real failure where loader ignores the resolved path and still opens `~/.config`. Integration-brake: no subprocess/network and no model output dependency; local filesystem/env wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory containing a known config file and place a different file under `~/.config`; run loader and assert values come from the XDG path. Concrete miss if only Unit: path resolver returns correct path but caller still reads default location.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundary: parsing env overrides and merge precedence with file config. Unit-testing merge precedence alone can miss incorrect `TOOL_` key mapping/coercion before merge. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior text is local config precedence only (no external process/network/AI).
behaviors.test_description: Create temp YAML with `database.host: file-host`, set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env-host`, run loader, assert effective config uses `env-host`. Concrete miss if only Unit: precedence function is correct, but env parser maps to wrong key and file value wins in real flow.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Need real interaction with YAML parser and loader error translation. Unit with mocked parser exception can hide failures where actual parser exception shape/fields differ and line number is lost. System rejected: no subprocess/network/OS-specific behavior beyond local file read; Agentic/Workflow rejected: no AI decision path.
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML in temp config (known failing line), run loader, assert failure message includes that line number. Concrete miss if only Unit: mocked exception always has `line`, but real parser raises a different structure and loader omits line info.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires module wiring plus real local filesystem effects (mkdir + write + subsequent load) in one process. Unit with mocked filesystem can miss concrete path construction/order bugs where defaults are written to wrong location or after load attempt. System rejected because no subprocess/network or OS eventing beyond ordinary local FS operations.
behaviors.test_description: Start with isolated temp HOME/XDG path where config dir is absent; run loader; assert directory now exists, defaults file exists at expected path, and loader proceeds using it. Concrete miss if only Unit: mocked fs confirms `mkdir`/`write` calls, but real path concatenation writes file to wrong directory so startup still fails.
